description_long: This is a medium-sized, open cardboard box with four flaps, three of which are upright and one fully extended outward. The box's exterior is dark brown, while the interior is a lighter tan color, both surfaces marked with splatters and smudges of blue, yellow, and purple paint, giving it a used and somewhat artistic appearance. The box appears slightly worn, with visible creases and some tape or label remnants on the outside. One of the side flaps is torn and hangs down, suggesting rough handling or frequent use. The interior is empty, revealing the corrugated cardboard texture and more paint stains. This object is typical of parcels found in urban environments, such as on sidewalks, in alleys, or near recycling bins.
description: A worn, open cardboard box with colorful paint splatters and one torn flap, showing signs of heavy use and urban wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open box with three upright flaps and a clear look at the colorful, paint-splattered interior.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the torn flap hanging down and the box's dark, slightly scuffed exterior.
description_view_2: Rear view with the long flap fully extended outward, revealing more of the box's interior paint stains.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the torn flap visible on the left and the box's worn, dark exterior.
